did:plc:ohjchth35p6z6wpc5dsorsta

data.body: Anyway, one thing I remember clearly is a scene where Henry VIII speaks on the Fisher Price toddler rotary phone with the Pope about his divorce, which the Pope is not inclines to grant.
data.author: did:plc:ohjchth35p6z6wpc5dsorsta

data.body:
Henry, in a fit of pique, whines and says “But I’m the King of England!” and the Pope replies, quite matter of factly “Well I’m the Pope of the whole
Goddamn world!”
data.author: did:plc:ohjchth35p6z6wpc5dsorsta

data.body: The only guy in our group played Henry, of course, and the rest of us played all the wives as well as all the other roles.
data.author: did:plc:ohjchth35p6z6wpc5dsorsta

data.body:
The whole thing was a delight and it won 1st prize in the Ball State Creative Writing contest later that summer.

We also made a great one about Nero where we set some of my brother’s legos on fire in Nero’s garage and filmed while he played “Smoke on the Water” on the violin.

So excited to get my hands on this book by @schuneke.bsky.social today! The fab In Bed With The Right podcast tuned me in to this work, and it resonates with my archival work on women convicted of political crimes in Stalinist Hungary. 

I love when works of history are also good reads, as this is.
